text:
First, note:  
- US/Eastern in 1859 would be roughly UTC−5 (no DST then).  
- Europe/Athens in 1859 would be LMT (UTC+1:34:52) but for simplicity, standard time used later was EET (UTC+2). I’ll use UTC+2 for Athens (though historically not formalized until 1895, but for this exercise, likely intended offset is +2).  

UTC = 2:00 (Mar 8) − (−5) = 07:00 UTC on Mar 8, 1859.  
Athens time = 07:00 + 2 = 09:00 on Mar 8, 1859.  

FINAL_ANSWER: 1859-03-08 09:00
